claim_boundary.next_gate: Submit a checked negative lemma artifact for at least one candidate, or falsify the registry by clearing R8 Route A or Route B.
claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: No candidate is a checked negative lemma. No R5 reroute, R1 solution, occurrence removal, proxy-T reduction, B7 credit, resource saving, or impossibility theorem is supported.
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: R10 turns R9 reroute pressure into five falsifiable negative-lemma candidates and five external PR packets.

acceptance_conditions: Bind the R7 contract hash and R8 preflight hash.
List each failed Route A/B predicate and prove the predicate cannot be cleared inside the covered domain.
State explicitly that the lemma is conditional on the current R7/R8 contract language.
candidate_negative_lemmas.covered_domain: The current R1 contract routes A and B under the R7 submission contract and R8 preflight predicates.
falsification_tests: Submit a target R1 artifact that passes Route A.
Submit a target R1 artifact that passes Route B.
Show an R8 predicate mismatch against the R7 contract.
candidate_negative_lemmas.title: Route-contract rejection lemma candidate

acceptance_conditions: Prove exact-pass count remains zero under the declared tolerance and grid.
candidate_negative_lemmas.covered_domain: All nonempty leave-out subsets of the five current line1381 off-grid parameters covered by R9 leave-out pressure.
falsification_tests: Find any leave-out subset with exact pass.
Show a missing nonempty subset among the 31 leave-out rows.
Show a tolerance or grid mismatch that invalidates a recorded exact failure.
candidate_negative_lemmas.title: Leave-out parameter elimination lemma candidate

acceptance_conditions: Prove total exact absorption count remains zero inside the covered arithmetic model.
candidate_negative_lemmas.covered_domain: Signed-grid context absorption attempts for widths 1 through 5 over the current line1381 parameter domain.
falsification_tests: Produce one exact absorption at width <= 5.
Expose an untested signed-combination family inside the stated width bound.
Lower the best context-grid error to zero under the same arithmetic model.
candidate_negative_lemmas.title: Bounded context absorption lemma candidate

acceptance_conditions: State the exact corridor reference family under coverage.
candidate_negative_lemmas.covered_domain: Best-context replay candidates under the current commutation-corridor references.
falsification_tests: Produce one candidate accepted by all corridor references.
Show a blocked corridor reference is unsound or outside the covered domain.
Replay a candidate that preserves the required references with nonzero accepted count.
candidate_negative_lemmas.title: Commutation-corridor replay lemma candidate

acceptance_conditions: Bind Route B failure under the same pricing ledger.
State that the lemma is ledger-conditional and does not rule out new physical evidence.
candidate_negative_lemmas.covered_domain: Current physical-pricing ledger used by R8/R9 for Route B.
falsification_tests: Reduce physical cost-minus-credit to <= 0 under the same ledger.
Show the selected CNOT credit or placeholder proxy-T pressure is priced incorrectly.
Submit a same-ledger artifact that clears Route B.
candidate_negative_lemmas.title: Physical Route-B deficit lemma candidate

decision.why: R10 makes the negative-lemma work concrete and falsifiable, but a candidate registry is still not a checked lemma.

external_pr_packets.required_output: machine-readable domain manifest plus human-readable scope note
external_pr_packets.title: Formalize the covered R1 domain

external_pr_packets.required_output: replay transcript binding R8/R9 hashes and source paths
external_pr_packets.title: Independently replay the R9 source hashes

external_pr_packets.required_output: checked or checkable proof skeleton with open obligations
external_pr_packets.title: Write a proof skeleton for one candidate

external_pr_packets.required_output: R1 artifact attempting to clear Route A or Route B
external_pr_packets.title: Attack the candidates with a route-clearing artifact

external_pr_packets.required_output: acceptance packet separating candidate, checked lemma, and reroute decision
external_pr_packets.title: Build the negative-lemma acceptance packet

requirements.label: R9 pressure is validation-clean and still blocks reroute
Registry contains five candidate negative lemmas
Candidates cover contract, parameter, context, commutation, and physical pressure families
Registry binds R8, R9, and R9 report source hashes
External PR packets separate theory, audit, compiler, formal, and maintainer work

evidence.why: R10 makes the negative-lemma work concrete and falsifiable, but a candidate registry is still not a checked lemma.

requirements.label: Registry explicitly refuses to upgrade candidates into checked lemmas
Registry preserves zero resource and B7 credit claims

evidence.next_gate: Submit a checked negative lemma for one or more R10 candidates, or falsify the registry by submitting an R1 artifact that clears R8 Route A or Route B.

requirements.label: Next gate is a checked lemma or an R1 Route A/B clearing artifact

title: B1/B7 Cone01 R10 R1 Negative-Lemma Candidate Registry Gate
